old_claim.note: Public Archives provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim_absent: No direct dependency remains because early mint quality control is represented through coinage, weights, dies, marks, and assay standards rather than a separate public archive institution.

source_shape.source_locator: The handbook's archaic and classical coinage chapters discuss Lydian/electrum coinage, civic minting, metal standards, denominations, dies, and coin production controls.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The source supports minting and weight/metal control, not a dependency on public archival repositories.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: Public records may document later mint administration, but treating public_archives as a prerequisite launders broad bureaucratic context into an early coin-quality-control node.

ontology_before: Coin Mint Assay Offices depended on Public Archives, implying civic record repositories enabled early mint quality control.
ontology_after: Coin Mint Assay Offices depends on metal coinage and portable weighing; public archives remain a neighboring administrative technology.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Changed: coin_mint_assay_offices no longer directly depends on public_archives.
would_reject_if: The node is later narrowed to mint record offices rather than assay and quality control.
A stronger source shows the earliest mint assay offices are attested specifically as archival offices.
A mint_accounting_records node is added and should sit between public_archives and later mint administration.
short_reason: Public archives are administrative context, not a direct early mint-control prerequisite.
